data.title: Wall Street snaps two-week losing streak, leaving S&P 500 just under record close
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) on Friday advanced 1.47% for the week to end at 6,114.63 points, posting gains in three out of five sessions. Its accompanying SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY) added 1.49% for the week.

The benchmark index snapped a two-week losing streak, and is now just a whisker away from its record closing high. Wall Street's premier stock average has been on a bit of a roller-coaster over the last two weeks, but after all that still finds itself up nearly 4% for the fledgling year.

Unsurprisingly, another week brought another round of headlines dominated by tariffs. U.S. President Donald Trump on Monday announced 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S.

"Imports of these products were close to $108bn in 2024 with tariffs averaging 4.3% on that trade," JPMorgan's Michael Feroli said on Friday. See the table he shared below:

"Consequently the aggregate impact of a 25% tariff on GDP is not that large, though it could clearly be important for those two industries, especially aluminum where imports are a larger share of the market. In both cases, Canada, China, and Mexico are the largest providers, especially Canada in the case of aluminum," Feroli added.

Then, on Thursday, Trump signed a memorandum that tasked several of his federal agencies with investigating current trade relations and proposing reciprocal tariffs on a country-specific basis. Still, the fact that Trump did not immediately put in effect the tit-for-tat tariffs and was scant on details, helped market sentiment.

The other big story of the week was economic data on inflation. The January consumer price index (CPI) report on Wednesday came in significantly hotter than expected, sending markets into a tailspin. Traders immediately moved to price in only one interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve this year.

But the producer price index (PPI) report on Thursday provided some relief. On the surface, it came in towards the hotter side. However, digging deeper, the components in the PPI that directly feed into the core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index came in soft for January. Most analysts now see annual growth in the January PCE deflator falling to its lowest reading since early 2021.

Turning to the weekly performance of the S&P 500 (SP500) sectors, nine of the 11 ended in positive territory. The heavyweight Technology sector rose the most, with an advance of nearly 4%. Health Care and Financials were the two losers. See below a breakdown of the performance of the sectors as well as their accompanying SPDR Select Sector ETFs from February 7 close to February 14 close:

#1: Information Technology +3.76%, and the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLK) +3.07%.

#2: Communication Services +1.99%, and the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC) +2.50%.

#3: Materials +1.75%, and the Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLB) +1.77%.

#4: Consumer Staples +1.73%, and the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLP) +1.59%.

#5: Energy +1.06%, and the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLE) +1.71%.

#6: Utilities +1.05%, and the Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLU) +1.23%.

#7: Consumer Discretionary +0.28%, and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLY) +0.63%.

#8: Real Estate +0.22%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLRE) +0.21%.

#9: Industrials +0.15%, and the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLI) +0.16%.

#10: Financials -0.08%, and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLF) -0.04%.

#11: Health Care -1.11%, and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLV) -1.11%.

For investors looking to track the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

For investors looking into the future of what's happening, take a look at the Seeking Alpha Catalyst Watch to see next week's breakdown of actionable events that stand out.

data.title: Intel is Hold rated across the board despite Trump upheaval
data.description:
This week, Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) posted a record rally after Vice President J.D. Vance highlighted the need to manufacture the most advanced AI chips in the U.S. in line with the Trump administration’s America First strategy.

His remarks, closely followed by a Baird report indicating a potential tie-up between the company and its main rival, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), sent Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares ~24% higher this week, its best weekly performance since 2000.

While the Trump administration has breathed new life into Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), its stock, having lost more than half of its value since December 2023 amid leadership uncertainty and declining sales, remains Hold rated among Wall Street and Seeking Alpha analysts.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha's Quant System, which consistently outperforms the market, has indicated an unchanged Hold position on INTC for three consecutive years, according to available data.

The Santa Clara, California-based chip giant lost a quarter of its market capitalization in August after the company, with its Q2 FY24 results, suspended dividends and announced layoffs impacting 15% of its staff amid a $10B cost-reduction plan.

In December, Intel (INTC) announced the abrupt departure of its CEO, Patrick Gelsinger, under whose leadership the company witnessed three consecutive years of sales contractions that led to its first full-year loss in 2024.

Despite Intel's (INTC) recent rally, Citi reaffirmed its Neutral rating on the stock on Thursday, citing multiple reasons why a joint venture between the company and TSMC will not succeed.

“Intel would be beholden to TSMC and different process ecosystems,” analyst Christopher Danely wrote, adding, “We view the success of this type of arrangement as unlikely” and “continue to believe Intel should exit the foundry business.”

This week, SA analyst Bay Area Ideas downgraded Intel (INTC) to Neutral from Buy, citing unfavorable technical indicators in its stock. “As Warren Buffett once stated, a cheap price for a mediocre business may not be a good deal, and I believe Intel is a good example of that currently.”

data.title: Will GameStop keep its retro vibe or pile billions into crypto?
data.description:
GameStop (NYSE:GME) posted a modest gain of 7% last week amid buzz over a possible entry back into the crypto sector. CNBC reported that the retailer is eyeing investments in alternative asset classes, including crypto, and especially bitcoin (BTC-USD).

Sources indicated that GameStop (NYSE:GME) is still figuring out if the investment is sensible for the business. The report caught the attention of investors because GME had $4.6 billion in cash holdings at the end of Q3, compared to its current market cap of $12.1 billion.

Investors may remember that GameStop (GME) has a mixed history with cryptocurrency and blockchain initiatives in general. The company launched a self-custodial cryptocurrency wallet in 2022 that allowed customers to manage digital assets, including cryptocurrencies and non-fungible tokens. The move was part of GameStop's (GME) broader strategy to expand into the digital asset space.

The Texas-based retailer also unveiled an NFT marketplace later in 2022 that was built on the Ethereum-based Loopring Layer 2 network. The company also partnered with Immutable X, an Ethereum-based Layer 2 scaling solution, to integrate its global order book into the GameStop (GME) NFT marketplace.

However, GameStop's (GME) adventures into the crypto world were cut short when the company decided to shut down its cryptocurrency wallet service in 2023. The official reason given at the time for the closure was "regulatory uncertainty" surrounding the crypto industry.

Ryan Cohen has said very little publicly about GameStop's (GME) plans for its cash hoard, which raised the level of intrigue after he posted a photo of him alongside MicroStrategy (MSTR) co-founder and major Bitcoin (BTC-USD) investor Michael Saylor. As for the company itself, most of its marketing and social media interactions this year have highlighted the upside of the physical videogame business, trading cards, and collectibles. The retro and nostalgic appeal to consumers has been unmistakable.

GameStop (GME) is not expected to report Q4 earnings until the middle part of March or later. Options trading implies a share price move of 9% or more before April. Short interest on GME stands at 7.2% of the total float.

data.title: Insider trades: Merck, McDonald's among notable names this week
data.description:
A weekly summary of insider trading highlighting a few notable buying and selling by investors, directors and executives. The trades have taken place between February 10, 2025 and February 14, 2025.

• Merck & Co’s (NYSE:MRK) EVP, Chief Info & Digital Officer, David Michael Williams, sold about 6,000 shares of common stock at a price of $85.34 per share for a total of $512,057. Williams now owns about 24,123 shares of the company.

• McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) disclosed a transaction by its Chairman and CEO Chief Chris Kempczinski, who sold 10,500 shares of common stock at a price of $ 310.05 per share, totaling $3.3M. Kempczinski now holds about 32,756 shares of the company.

• Rahul Samant, EVP & Chief Info Officer at Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) disclosed through a filing that he sold 38,023 shares of the company at a price of $64.89 per share for total worth $2.5M. Samant now holds 52,789 shares of the company.

• Stephen Squeri, Chairman and CEO at American Express Company (NYSE:AXP) disclosed through a filing that he sold 50,841 shares of the company at a price of $319.45 per share for total worth $16.2M. Squeri now holds 92,810 shares of the company.

• Phil Guido, EVP & Chief Commercial Officer at Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) disclosed a transaction this week. Guido purchased 4,645 shares at a price of $ 107.56 per share for a total value of $499,616. Guido now holds 33,522 shares of the company.

• Ronald E Blaylock, Director at Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) bought 19,457 shares at a price of $25.65 per share for a total value of $499,072. Blaylock now holds 27,707 shares of the company.

• Berkshire Hathaway, disclosed a transaction at Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) this week. It purchased 763,017 shares at a price of $46.82 per share for a total of $35.7M. Berkshire Hathaway now owns about 264.9M shares of the company.

data.title: Blackstone, Corsair Capital looking to sell off First Eagle: report
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) and Corsair Capital are looking to sell First Eagle Investment Management for more than $4B, the Financial Times reported late Friday, citing people briefed on the matter.

According to the report, Morgan Stanley has been hired to lead the sale, the people said.

The firms acquired First Eagle in 2015 for $4B.

The FT said Blackstone and Corsair are looking to take advantage of the takeover wave in the asset management segment as private-equity and financial-services firms are looking to get further into fee-based financial businesses.

The people were quoted as saying that First Eagle generates around $500 million in annual earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization.

Neither Blackstone nor Corsair immediately responded to requests for comment by Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy calls for creation of European army
data.description:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Saturday called for the creation of "armed forces of Europe," calling for increased European self-sufficiency, citing wavering American support for the continent.

"I really believe that time has come. The armed forces of Europe must be created so that Europe’s future depends only on Europeans," said Zelenskyy at the Munich Security Conference in Germany.

The "Americans might say no to Europe on issues that threaten it," added Zelenskyy.

Zelenskyy warned Russia was "not preparing for dialogue" to end the current war. He said Ukraine's three-year fight against Russia proved that a foundation exists for the often-discussed European army already exists.

The Ukrainian President met with U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance on Friday to discuss the possibility of a Russia-Ukraine peace deal, but asked for "security guarantees" before any talks can progress.

In an interview with NBC News on Friday, Zelenskyy said it would be "very, very, very difficult" for Ukraine to survive without U.S. military support, both currently and in the future once the war ends.

U.S. President Donald Trump has repeatedly called for an agreement with Ukraine to access its rare earth minerals in return for the U.S. aiding the country's defense against Russia.

The U.S. has asked European nations to provide proposals on the weaponry, peacekeeping troops and security arrangements they could provide Ukraine with as part of any security guarantees to end its war with Russia, the Financial Times reported Saturday

According to the FT, Washington intends to gauge Europe's willingness to protect Kyiv after a settlement with Russia and scope out the price Europe is willing to pay to be involved in the talks with Moscow.

Here are some popular oil-focused exchange-traded funds: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

data.title: Uber sues DoorDash over food-delivery business practices: reports
data.description:
Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) has sued DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) in a California state court, accusing the larger food-delivery player of anticompetitive business practices, according to media reports.

In the lawsuit filed Friday, the reports said, Uber alleged DoorDash has made it harder and costly for restaurants to sign up with more than one delivery service.

The lawsuit reportedly says those practices help DoorDash to charge higher fees to its customers for “lower-quality service.”

“Restaurants simply cannot afford to stand up to DoorDash, and find themselves powerless to choose the service or services that are best for their businesses in the market for first-party delivery,” Business Insider quoted the suit as saying. "Uber's restaurant-customers have reported feeling like they have a 'gun to their head,' that DoorDash is a 'monopolist,' and that they are being bullied by DoorDash. But most restaurants have no meaningful option to resist DoorDash, given the power it wields through the DoorDash App in Third-Party Delivery."

The suit reportedly says DoorDash’s alleged anticompetitive practices have cost Uber millions because of terminated and possible revenue streams.

Uber is seeking unspecified damages and for an order by the court to require DoorDash to change its business practices, the reports said.

“Uber’s case has no merit. Their claims are unfounded and based on their inability to offer merchants, consumers, or couriers a quality alternative,” DoorDash told Seeking Alpha in a statement.

data.title: Upstart, Robinhood post biggest weekly gains among financial stocks; WD, KNSL slide
data.description:
In the heat of earnings season, this week's biggest gainers among financial stocks included predominantly fintech names, while insurers largely fell the most.

In all, the Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF) slipped 0.1% for the week ended Feb. 14, falling short of the broader S&P 500's 1.5% rise.

Rising the most of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B), Upstart Holdings (UPST) gapped up 20% in the past week on the back of strong Q4 results and guidance, which was followed by several analyst upgrades on the stock;

Robinhood Markets (HOOD), which during the week also posted a blowout fourth quarter, jumped 17%;

Lufax Holding (LU), a China-based consumer finance company, also surged about 17%;

Property and casualty insurer Palomar Holdings (PLMR) gained 16% after turning better-than-expected Q4 results; and

Futu Holdings (FUTU) rounded up the five biggest winners with a 15% climb.

On the negative side, commercial and residential mortgage finance company Walker & Dunlop (WD) took the lead, retreating 8.4%, after delivering mixed Q4 results;

Kinsale Capital Group (KNSL) slid 8.2% even after the P&C insurer surpassed Q4 top and bottom line estimates;

White Mountain Insurance Group (WTM) shed 7.9% despite Q4 earnings and revenue topping expectations;

Markel Group (MKL), which during the week published stronger-than-forecast Q4 profit, slumped 7.8%; and

Argentine bank Grupo Financiero Galicia (GGAL) dipped 7.5%.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Snap, Peloton and Skyworks stocks among top picks
data.description:
The S&P500 (SP500) closed in the red on Friday, after headlines on Donald Trump’s tariff policy and a hotter-than-expected U.S. consumer inflation report in January grabbed the limelight.

For the week, Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gained 1.2%, while Dow (DJI) rose 0.5%.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Skyworks downgraded at Argus on Apple worries

Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) was downgraded to Hold from Buy by Argus, amid worries about its Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) business following its latest quarterly results. The company reported that Apple plans to reduce its dependence on Skyworks for its iPhone 17 model.

“Apple is by far the company's largest customer, at 69% of FY24 revenue. At mid-percentage, Skyworks will lose an estimate $600 million-plus in total annual revenue due to iPhone content loss. Skyworks in spring 2024 announced that it had lost approximately 10% of content in the iPhone 16 family,” said Argus analysts.

Benchmark initiates coverage on Tesla, Lucid with Bull rating

Benchmark started coverage on Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID) with Buy rating.

The brokerage thinks Tesla has multiple opportunities to fuel growth, including autonomous vehicles, robotics, and energy generation/storage. "Advancements in manufacturing and battery technology have driven down market prices and we see the release of more affordable Tesla models in 2025 as a key catalyst for the stock," highlighted analyst Mickey Legg.

Meanwhile, Lucid is believed to be well positioned to achieve significant market share based on its advanced technology, well-stocked balance sheet, access to capital, award-winning vehicles, Saudi investment, partnerships and highly integrated manufacturing capabilities.

Argus positive on Peloton’s new CEO, cost-savings measures

Argus upgraded Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) to Buy from Hold, saying the company’s new CEO, increased interest in fitness equipment, and cost-savings measures should contribute to a narrower loss in FY25.

“Given his background in the health & fitness industry, we think that Peloton’s new CEO, Peter Stern, can reduce debt, revive subscriber growth, and increase customer retention,” said analyst John Staszak and set a $15 price target on the stock.

The combination of cost-cutting and raised EBITDA led Staszak to narrow his FY25 loss estimate to $0.40 from $0.45 and FY26 loss estimate to $0.25 from $0.30 per share.

Guggenheim cautious on Snap, sees profitability pressures ahead

Guggenheim downgraded Snap (NYSE:SNAP) to Neutral from Buy, saying that the company has well-defined broad goals like increasing user engagement, developing more effective lower-funnel advertising, and improving brand advertising growth, but the effective investment in and execution towards these goals is likely to remain elusive in 2025.

The brokerage also believes that the company’s plan to invest more to be competitive drives further pressure on profitability and cut PT to $11 from $13.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) was added to the Tactical Outperform list at Evercore, ahead of its fiscal fourth-quarter results. Analyst Mark Lipacis said Nvidia is still the platform of choice for hyperscalers' customers, with it being 5 to 10 years ahead of "anything else in the market."

GlobalFoundries (NASDAQ:GFS) was upgraded by Needham to Buy from Hold after the company's quarterly results and outlook indicated potential for a strong gross margin ramp. “Not only do we forecast a return to Y/Y growth in 2025, but also we see meaningful gross margin expansion through the year,” said analyst N. Quinn Bolton.

First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) received an upgrade at Mizuho to Outperform from Neutral with a $259 price target, hiked from $218, citing a materially improved sales outlook after 2026 and a stronger competitive position in the U.S. market.

Morgan Stanley upgraded Upstart Holdings (NASDAQ:UPST) to Equal-weight from Underweight, as analyst James Faucette is encouraged by a favorable environment from both a funding and macro perspective for the lending platform after the company issued robust Q4 earnings and introduced strong 2025 guidance.

data.title: Bulls vs. Bears: Can Super Micro Computer sustain post-earnings rally
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) climbed ~32% this week even after the company missed the consensus with its preliminary financials for Q2 fiscal 2025 but addressed its regulatory concerns, prompting Wall Street to take note of its post-earnings rally.

With the San Jose, California-based AI server company having added ~57% in value this year, contrasting its underperformance last year, many Seeking Alpha analysts also reevaluated their positions on Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) this week.

The Bulls

“A massive short-covering taking place in the Super Micro Computer stock after-hours trading: Bears started to panic,” wrote SA Investing Group Leader Danil Sereda in “Super Micro Computer: Shorts Cover, Time To Buy (Rating Upgrade).” “The downward pressure may be finally coming to an end now, so I'm upgrading the stock back to "Buy".”

“Super Micro reported strong 2Q25 results, easing investor concerns about Nasdaq delisting and showing solid sales growth despite softening margins,” added SA analyst On the Pulse in “Super Micro Computer: Sentiment Is Turning (Upgrade).” “Taking into account that the IT company is on track to file its overdue financial reports, I am willing to modify my stock classification for Super Micro to ‘Hold.’”

“Super Micro Computer, Inc. reported mixed Q2 results but remains bullish on 2025, driven by Nvidia Blackwell products and AI infrastructure spending,” argued SA Investing Group Leader James Foord in “Super Micro Computer Q2 Results: It's The Final Countdown.” “SMCI's stock could either soar to $100 or plummet to $10, making it a high-risk, high-reward investment.”

“I was initially very concerned about whether SMCI buyers could hold the pivotal November bottom, which is obviously a hugely important level to defend,” noted SA Investing Group Leader JR Research in “Super Micro Computer: Why A Breakout Move Higher Could Surprise The Bears.”

“Not only did they confirm compliance filings are on track for February 2025, but they also delivered better-than-expected financials,” added SA analyst Bashar Issa in “Super Micro's Big Reveal.” “FY2026 revenue projected to reach $40B, signaling confidence in long-term demand.”

The Bears

“Coupled with looming delisting and liquidity risks, we believe there’s limited durability to Super Micro Computer, Inc. stock’s post-earnings rally,” noted Livy Investment Research in “Super Micro Computer Q2 Earnings: Differentiating Facts From Distraction.” “Volatility is likely to remain the near-term theme for the stock until it becomes current with its outstanding financial statement filings, delisting and liquidity risks subside, and fundamentals reaccelerate.”

“Super Micro Computer reported mediocre preliminary Q2/FY2025 results and issued third quarter guidance below consensus expectations,” argued Henrik Alex in “Super Micro Computer: Stellar FY2026 Sales Projection Could Prove Overly Ambitious - Sell.” “I remain skeptical regarding the company's ability to live up to management's aggressive sales projections given anticipated slower growth at key GPU supplier Nvidia and potential competitive pressures.”

“Preliminary results continue to show declining revenue growth and gross margins,” wrote SA analyst Kumquat Research in “Super Micro Computer Earnings: Don't Chase This Gamble.” “Until audited financial results are available, investors should stay away from SMCI stock.”
